original: Stray tabby cat Larry becomes Chief Mouser to the Cabinet Office at 10 Downing Street, the official resident of the UK Prime Minister, from Battersea Dogs & Cats Home
Rocket-powered ice sled attains 399 kph, Lake George, New York
First teddy bear introduced in America, made by Morris and Rose Michtom
First draft of the complete human genome is published in the journal "Nature"
Irena Sendler [née Krzyżanowska], Polish nurse, social worker, and WWII heroine who helped smuggle Jewish ghetto children to safety, born in Warsaw, Russian Poland (d. 2008)[1]
Miep Gies, Dutch discoverer of Anne Frank's diary and friend of the Frank family, born in Vienna, Austria-Hungary (d. 2010)

As a member of the gold medal-winning US four-man bobsleigh team at Lake Placid,Eddie Eaganbecomes the only Olympian to win gold medals at both the Summer (boxing gold in Antwerp 1920) and Winter Games in different sports
American speed skaterEric Heidenwins 500m in Olympic record 38.03s; first of record 5 gold medal sweep of events at Lake Placid, New York
Walt Disney's animated film "Cinderella" premieres in Boston. Massachusetts
Norman Bridwell, American author and cartoonist (Clifford the Big Red Dog), born in Kokomo, Indiana (d. 2014)
NHL OilersWayne Gretzkyassists on NHL-record-tying 7 goals, in 8-2 victory over Washington at Northlands Coliseum, Edmonton
Troy State sets NCAA Div II record with 103 points in 2nd half routing DeVry Institute 187-117
Wenona Mustangs beat Toluca Wildcats in Illinois Basketball Tournament in 10 overtimes, 26-22
Wayne Gretzkyrecords 5 assists in NY Rangers' 7-4 win over the Predators; takes a 1,000 point lead over Gordie Howe in all time NHL scoring lead; Gretzky's only appearance in Nashville
Great Britain’s 0-0 tie with the US is enough to clinch their first ever Olympic ice hockey gold medal at the Garmisch-Partenkirchen Winter Games; breaks Canada’s 4 straight Games golden streak
Longest dream (REM sleep) on record is experienced by Bill Carskadon in Chicago lasting 2 hours and 23 minutes
Friendsville Academy (Tenn) ends 138-game basketball losing streak
The Angel of the North, a large-scale steel sculpture 20 m (66 ft) tall by Antony Gormley is installed at Gateshead, northern England
Teen film "The Breakfast Club" is released, written and directed by John Hughes, starring Molly Ringwald, Judd Nelson, Emilio Estevez, Anthony Michael Hall and Ally Sheedy
President-electFranklin D. Rooseveltsurvives assassination in Miami, Florida, attempt but Chicago Mayor Anton Cermak is mortally wounded, he would die on March 29
Leon SpinksbeatsMuhammad Aliin 15 for heavyweight boxing title
Schooner 'Fram' reaches latitude 78° 41' S, the farthest south ever by a ship
Niklaus Wirth, Swiss computer scientist who invented nine programming languages (Pascal, Oberon) and won the Turing Award in 1984, born in Winterthur, Switzerland (d. 2024)[1]
Walt Disney releases "Return to Never Land", an animated film sequel to 1953's "Peter Pan"
Art Spiegelman, American cartoonist (The New Yorker, 1992-2002), Pulitzer Prize winning graphic novelist (Maus), and editor (Arcade; Raw), born in Stockholm, Sweden
The Beatles' "Meet the Beatles!" album goes #1 & stays #1 for 11 weeks

John Lennonand his wife Cynthia, andGeorge Harrison, his wife Pattie, and sister-in-law Jenny, travel to India to study Transcendental Meditation for two months withMaharishi Mahesh Yogiat his ashram in Rishikesh; Paul McCartney and Ringo Starr join them a few days later, Ringo only stays for 2 weeks
Soviet cross-country skier Raisa Smetanina claims her 3rd of 4 career Olympic gold medals in 5k event at Lake Placid; also wins 10k and relay gold (Innsbruck 1976) and relay (Albertville 1992)
ABA basketball Anaheim Amigos Les Salvage scores 10 3-point baskets in game vs Denver Rockets
First US female world figure skating champion (Tenley Albright)
-60°F (-51°C), Parshall, North Dakota (state record)
Biathlon 4 x 7.5k relay debuts at the Grenoble Winter Olympics with the Soviet Union taking the gold medal ahead of Norway and Sweden
Duke Ellingtonand his Orchestra first record "Take the 'A' Train" written by Billy Strayhorn, at RCA Studio in Hollywood, California; it goes on to become his signature tune
Johann Strauss' "Blue Danube" waltz premieres in Vienna
Willy Vandersteen, Belgian cartoonist (Suske & Wiske), born in Antwerp, Belgium (d. 1990)
Harvey Korman, American actor (The Carol Burnett Show; Blazing Saddles; High Anxiety), born in Chicago, Illinois (d. 2008)
John Helliwell, British rock Saxophonist (Supertramp - "The Logical Song"), born in Todmorden, West Riding of Yorkshire, England
Aleksandr Serebrov, Soviet cosmonaut (Soyuz T-7, T-8, TM-8, TM-17), born in Moscow, USSR (d. 2013)
Roger B Chaffee, American US Navy Lt Cmdr and NASA astronaut (Apollo1), born in Grand Rapids Michigan (d. 1967)
Ole Ellefsæter, Norwegian cross-country skier (Olympic gold 50km & 4×10km relay 1968; World C'ship gold 4×10km relay 1966), born in Furnes, Norway (d. 2022)
Brian Holland, American pianist, songwriter, and producer (Holland–Dozier–Holland - "Heat Wave"; "Where Did Our Love Go"; "You Keep Me Hanging On"), born in Detroit, Michigan
Michel Talagrand, French mathematician, 2024 Abel Prize for probability theory and describing randomness, born in Béziers, France[1]
Pumla Gobodo-Madikizela, South African psychologist, researcher and writer (A Human Being Died that Night), born in Langa, South Africa
Totò [Antonio Clemente], Italian actor (Motorizzate, Noi Duri) commonly referred to as the most popular Italian comedian of all time, born in Naples, Italy (d. 1967)
William Henry Pickering, American astronomer (9th & 10th moons of Saturn), born in Boston, Massachusetts (d. 1938)
William Parsons, Earl of Rosse, 1st uses 72 inch (183 cm) reflector telescope  at Birr Castle, Parsonstown, County Offaly, Ireland
First pilot plant to produce synthetic man-made diamonds is announced
Black abolitionists invade Boston courtroom rescuing a fugitive slave
23 cm (9 inches) of snow falls on New Orleans
Australia beat West Indies 2-1 in one of best Test Cricket series ever
Australia beat South Africa in cricket by an innings in 5 hrs 53 min playing time
Red Maple Leaf Flag becomes the official flag of Canada
Ken Lynch records "Misery," 1st Lennon-McCartney song by someone else
Sound recordings receive US federal copyright protection for the first time
USSR launches Prognoz 3 to study the Sun (589/200,300 km)
Paul Shirley (21) of Australia, sucked a lifesaver for 4 hrs 40 mins
Population of People's Republic of China hits 1.2 billion
Christopher Columbuswrites the first account of his first voyage to the Americas in a letter to his patron Luis de Santángel, Treasurer of Aragon, who then publishes it
Marconibegins regular broadcasting transmissions from Essex
Silent film "It" is released in the US starringClara Bow
